channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 3 blocked drains, 4th June
item.description:
3 x drains outside 15a Page Heath Lane blocked with leaves and mud causing flooding to adjacent homes
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Waldo Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 2 x drains on Bird In Hand Lane / Page Heath Lane both blocked with mud and leaves causing flooding, 4th June
item.description:
2 drains on Bird In Hand Lane / Page Heath Lane both blocked with mud and leaves causing flooding.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Page Heath Lane, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Still small rubbish in flowerbeds, 4th June
item.description:
Appreciate some work has been done on cleaning the street and flower beds, but there is still so much small rubbish in the flower beds - cigarette butts, bottle tops, small bits of plastic etc. Please please properly clean up the flower bed - particularly the middle flower bed just behind the seats.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Liverpool Grove, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rainton Road - full of litter, 4th June
item.description:
Hi, Please clean this street asap. It's constantly full of litter. Best, L

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: This area is persistent to fly tipping! The area needs to be properly maintained., 4th June
item.description:

item.title: Lots of litter, 4th June
item.description:
There is lots of litter next to Alan's Plants on the corner - area indicated in the pictures. Please remove it, this us third time I'm reporting this.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping next to Old Fire Station Apartments, 4th June
item.description:
Hi there, Please remove this fly-tipping. This is a common area for it and should be monitored regularly or have CCTV installed. Thanks, L

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Failed asphalt, 4th June
item.description:
Adding photo as requested. Houses are shaking when heavy vehicles drive past

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, Sherborne Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Roof Damage and Water Ingress in Church Hall Caused by Falling Branch from Churchyard Tree, 4th June
item.description:
Recent high winds have brought down a branch from one of two tall trees on edge of churchyard, damaging church hall roof. Rain has penetrated through to the main hall used on a regular community basis , including daily pre school. Repair to roof quoted at £2, 500. Serious concerns over safety to all using this main entrance and path to our church hall from these trees. Trees inspected in May 2025. Require urgent pruning please.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Motor bike chained to lamp post causing a trip hazard, 4th June
item.description:
I would like to report a potential trip hazard on the public pavement. A motorcycle has been parked fully on the road and secured to a lamppost using a heavy chain. The chain is looped around the base of the lamppost and runs along the ground, leaving approximately a 30cm slack gap between the post and the bike. This chain is positioned close to the kerb and sits at ground level, creating a risk that pedestrians—particularly small children, dog walkers or poor visibility—could trip over it. Although there is space to pass, there is also space to pass through the gap between the chains and the low positioning and slack of the chain could makes it difficult to see and avoid, especially in low light conditions. I am concerned this presents a genuine safety hazard on the public pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 50, Stuart Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yes, 4th June
item.description:
Graffiti from local gangs all over the bus shelter please clean it off before they put more.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 245, Kilburn Lane, Queen's Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (offensive)

item.title: Tree in grass verge has died, 4th June
item.description:
Tree on grass verge has died. Nearest address is 16 Townshend Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Townshend Road, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branches falling AGAIN, 4th June
item.description:
This is not the first time I have raised concerns about these trees. I have previously reported large branches falling from them and provided photographs showing the risk they posed the last report within the last month. I was advised that the trees had been inspected and were considered safe, with no pruning or maintenance required literally within the last month. However, yet another substantial branch has now fallen from one of these trees today. I have attached photographs showing the size of the branch. It is large enough that, had it struck a person, vehicle, or property, it could have caused serious injury or damage. Given that I have raised concerns before and that large branches continue to fall, I do not believe it is reasonable to continue classifying these trees as presenting no risk. The repeated failure of sizeable branches suggests that the trees require further assessment and appropriate maintenance, including pruning where necessary. Please review urgently by a qualified tree surgeon and that a full inspection of both trees is carried out. Please also confirm what action will be taken to prevent further incidents and why no action was taken previously. I am increasingly concerned that if no action is taken and someone is injured or property is damaged, the consequences could have been avoided had the repeated reports and evidence been acted upon. Clearly the last report was incorrectly assessed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 91, Petersham Drive, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown shrubs and bushes, 4th June
item.description:
Overgrown bushes and shrubs along the entire perimeter of school and sports hall is causing access issues for pedestrians. Unable to use the foot path as per photo. Whilst this may not be responsible of Council the school is the responsibility of local council. Please arrange to inspect all of the grounds and get the overgrowth cut back. This is a yearly issue that has to be reported to request cutting. The school and council should be actively monitoring the vegetation and cut back regularly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hurst Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On the plants in st marks square hundreds of box tree caterpillars on the box trees planted there, 4th June
item.description:
Waiting outside hotel noticed the planted box trees are covered in box tree caterpillars there’s hundreds of of them on the plants these caterpillars can cause irritation to the skin if touched need to be destroyed
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Newbury Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree obstructing pavement, 4th June
item.description:
Tree shoots are obstructing the pavement and need cutting back. This tree is on a school run.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Ormiston Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet
